claim: Specification prompts (detailed, structured plans) enable AI coding models to scale work dramatically—moving from 200-token simple prompts to 1,600+ token complex multi-feature prompts with increasing reliability
actionable: When using o1 or o3 models, invest time upfront writing detailed specification documents with objective, context, and low-level tasks; this unlocks far more complex work than ad-hoc prompting
source_title: o1 Architect: AI Coding LIMIT TESTING to prepare for o3-mini (Aider + Deepseek)

claim: Using o1 as architect and a smaller model (DeepSeek, Claude) as editor creates an effective chain that balances expensive reasoning with faster execution, reducing token costs
actionable: For large coding tasks, structure your Aider commands as: o1 in architect mode for planning/design, then use a cheaper editor model for implementation; this balances performance and cost
source_title: o1 Architect: AI Coding LIMIT TESTING to prepare for o3-mini (Aider + Deepseek)

claim: o1 reasoning model struggles with very large prompts (1600+ tokens on complex codebases with 15+ files); this is the edge of current reasoning capabilities before task decomposition or context chunking becomes necessary
actionable: For prompts exceeding 1000 tokens on large codebases, split work into independent subtasks or use multiple agent runs rather than attempting all changes in one massive specification
source_title: o1 Architect: AI Coding LIMIT TESTING to prepare for o3-mini (Aider + Deepseek)

claim: Spec prompts serve as reusable assets—you can deploy the same detailed plan to multiple AI agents or redeploy to a different agent if initial results are incomplete
actionable: Write your specification documents as standalone files independent of any single AI tool; this lets you reuse and iterate on plans across different agents without rewriting
source_title: o1 Architect: AI Coding LIMIT TESTING to prepare for o3-mini (Aider + Deepseek)

claim: The major limitation with current AI models is not model capability but rather prompt quality and context management; improving prompts scales output more than waiting for model upgrades
actionable: Focus engineering effort on crafting better specification prompts and managing context windows rather than hoping for the next model release to solve your problems
source_title: o1 Architect: AI Coding LIMIT TESTING to prepare for o3-mini (Aider + Deepseek)

claim: Reverting codebases between feature iterations tests model reliability and demonstrates how much work a single prompt can accomplish when backed by clear planning
actionable: Use git worktrees or resets to test your AI prompts multiple times in isolation; this reveals whether success is repeatable or luck-dependent
source_title: o1 Architect: AI Coding LIMIT TESTING to prepare for o3-mini (Aider + Deepseek)

claim: Clear component declaration syntax (type annotations, prop lists) in prompts makes it easier for AI models to understand and generate correct code structure
actionable: When describing UI components in prompts, use consistent pseudo-code declarations showing props, types, and structure rather than natural language descriptions
source_title: o1 Architect: AI Coding LIMIT TESTING to prepare for o3-mini (Aider + Deepseek)

claim: The prompt is becoming the fundamental unit of knowledge work in the AI era; learning to write powerful prompts is as important as learning a programming language
actionable: Treat prompt engineering as a core engineering discipline; invest time mastering specification prompts, context management, and evaluation patterns
source_title: o1 Architect: AI Coding LIMIT TESTING to prepare for o3-mini (Aider + Deepseek)
